name: ETH 4h Range Failure Reacceptance v1
edge_mechanism: a multi-bar breakout loses acceptance, value is reclaimed, and price rotates back through balance instead of continuing
thesis: Cycle 86 wants range-failure reversal after a multi-bar breakout loses acceptance and re-enters balance. ETH 4h is the cleanest pure-chop lane in the basket and therefore the best structural home for this concept. The last five cycles say sparse 4h continuation theater is dead, so this spec leans into a recurring failed-excursion mechanism that should trade more often than heroic breakout rescue logic.
invalidation_condition: If ETH 4h reacceptance events still undertrade or fail to rotate meaningfully after reclaiming balance, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price spends multiple bars outside a recent range, loses acceptance there, re-enters balance, and rotates back through the range.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Pure chop regimes are dominated by failed excursions more than durable breaks. Waiting for value reacceptance should give a structurally grounded reversal instead of blind fading.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is the best fit because it is the clearest chop-owned lane in the basket and should reward failed range extensions more than continuation logic.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: A real breakout can be misread as failed acceptance, causing repeated fades into actual directional continuation.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries trade but average win size is too small, extend the runner before adding more entry filters.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Repeated modest balance rotations, many first-target wins, and occasional fuller unwind through the range.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: ETH 4h clears moderate density and produces materially better participation than the recent sparse reclaim families.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-15 trades, or repeated reacceptance entries that immediately re-break the failed side.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: acceptable because failed multi-bar excursions recur in chop

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit pure-chop lane for range-failure reacceptance reversal.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h shows enough reacceptance rotations with positive first-target expectancy.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h remains too sparse or too shallow to monetize.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate count with many balance rotations.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same failed-break logic survives on a weaker but still allowed cross-lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: AXS 4h keeps a tradable version of the same reacceptance mechanism.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: AXS 4h instability destroys the mean-reversion thesis.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer but larger range-failure reversals than ETH.

validation_basket.why_included: Useful control to confirm that the idea is chop-owned and not trend-friendly.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO 4h still shows some failed excursions snapping back to value cleanly.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h confirms the concept belongs on chop lanes only.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower count, stronger but rarer reversals than ETH.

long: Exit remaining 20% on loss of VWAP or 5 bars
short: Exit remaining 20% on reclaim of VWAP or 5 bars
